raw_recent_text: "Ugh, not again," you spout as the old wagon shutters, then dies.
Stranded on the road again, but this time, your money has gone dry.
All but your 50 dollar bill that Grandma gave you on her last Christmas.
You've been saving it for when you needed it most, but surely, it won't be enough.
The door creaks open, and you step out into the night sky, coughing up the smoke from your fried vehicle.
After pushing your car off the road and between the trees, there isn't much else left for you to do,
so you begin to wander down the dark, lonely street.
But at the end of the road, where concrete turned to stone turned to dirt, you notice a light up ahead, on the top of a hill.
As you waltz into the old, wooden shack, your eyes begin to light up with the fire of a thousand suns.
Roulette wheels! Poker tables! And in a dark corner of the abandoned casino, sits a dealer, shuffling cards for a new round of Blackjack.
That 50 dollars might just come in handy after all. Thanks, Grandma!
As you go to sit down at the table, you hear the Dealer cough, then watch as he sits up.
In a deep, and yet strained voice, the Dealer, cloaked in darkness, poses a question to you.
Would you like to play a game of Blackjack?
You have $50

"If time is a circle, then are we all just rolling along like doughnuts in a bakery of eternity? Beware of the square bagels, they're time travelers in disguise."
Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 2 ~ ~ ~  True
You wake up smiling. When's the last time that happened? You can't remember, but it happened today.
The dream is about water. Rising water. You're in the car and it's filling up. First your feet. Then your lap. Then your chest.
You can't open the doors. The windows are locked. The water is warm, which is somehow worse.
You gasp awake. Bone dry. Heart hammering. You open the car door just to prove you can.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
Another night. Another dance with chance.

"Howdy, partner! The name's Jameson. Davey Jameson.
"I happen to notice you were admiring my steed. He's a beauty, isn't he. "
"You see, it's common courtesy when a cowboy rides by, "
to give their mighty steed a carrot, as a way to express your gratitude for their hardwork and commitment to the job."
"You my friend, you are carrotless. That's quite a disrespectful showing towards my steed. "
"My, my, this can't do at all. What if another cowboy comes by, you're just gonna disrespect their steed, too? "
"Tell you what, I happen to have one spare carrot in my pouch. Take this, and be ready. "
"You never know when a cowboy's gonna come trotting by."
Davey Jameson hands you his Carrot, and smiles.
"See, with this carrot in your possession, you're ready for anytime a cowboy strolls on down this road. "
"Just give their steed a carrot, and they'll be very grateful."
And with that, Jameson reins his horse high into the air, gives you a yee-haw, then dashes off down the road.
You dream of winning. The moment the million hits your account.
Confetti. Champagne. People cheering your name.
The Dealer shakes your hand. His grip is warm. He says, "I always knew you'd make it."
You wake up smiling. Then you check your balance. Still not there.
But soon. Soon.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
Come, sit down, we have a game to play.

run_summary.death_cause: Wrong place. Wrong time. Hostage situation gone wrong.
